title: Gentianella gageoides
taxonomy: Gentianella gageoides is a species of flowering plant in the family Gentianaceae. This plant is a member of the tribe Gentianeae, which is a group of flowering plants characterized by their five-lobed corolla. Gentianella gageoides is further classified within the genus Gentianella, which is a group of small to medium-sized annual or perennial plants. The plant's classification is supported by its morphological and molecular characteristics, which are consistent with other members of the Gentianaceae family. As a result, Gentianella gageoides is classified as Gentianella gageoides (Torr.) Greene.

intro: Gentianella gageoides, also known as Gage's gentian, is a small, annual or perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the gentian family. Traditionally, it has been used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, fever, and respiratory infections, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties. The bioactive constituents of Gage's gentian include iridoids, such as gentiopicroside and swertiamarin, as well as other compounds like gentisin, sweroside, and gentianin, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Gage's gentian is commonly prepared as a tincture, decoction, or infusion to treat digestive issues, and is also used in combination with other herbs to enhance its effects.
